title: Volunteer with EpiPulse
text:
EpiPulse is a public health initiative committed to evidence-based action. We rely on passionate volunteers to help us achieve our mission of improving health outcomes through education, research, and community engagement.



Make a Real Impact: Contribute to meaningful public health projects that directly benefit communities
Skill Development: Gain experience in public health, research, communication, and project management
Networking: Connect with like-minded professionals and experts in the field
Flexible Commitment: Choose roles that fit your schedule and interests
Recognition: Get certificates and public acknowledgment for your contributions




We have several volunteer positions available. Each role has specific requirements, expectations, and benefits. Please review the details below and apply if you meet the eligibility criteria.


Role Description: Create educational content, blog posts, social media materials, and resources for our website and platforms.
Expectations: - Produce 2-4 pieces of content per month - Research and fact-check information - Collaborate with the team on content strategy - Meet deadlines and quality standards
Benefits: - Writing portfolio development - Public health knowledge enhancement - Byline credits on published work - Certificate of contribution
Eligibility Criteria: - Strong writing and communication skills - Interest in public health topics - Access to computer and internet - Basic knowledge of public health concepts (preferred)



Role Description: Provide innovative ideas for projects, campaigns, and initiatives. Help develop strategies for program implementation.
Expectations: - Attend monthly brainstorming sessions - Submit 1-2 ideas per month - Participate in strategy discussions - Help refine and implement selected ideas
Benefits: - Strategic thinking skills development - Leadership experience - Networking with team members - Certificate of contribution
Eligibility Criteria: - Creative thinking and problem-solving skills - Interest in public health innovation - Good communication skills - Experience in project planning (preferred)



Role Description: Organize and conduct community health activities, workshops, and awareness campaigns.
Expectations: - Plan and execute 1-2 activities per quarter - Coordinate with local partners and stakeholders - Manage logistics and participant engagement - Report on activity outcomes
Benefits: - Event management experience - Community engagement skills - Leadership opportunities - Certificate of contribution
Eligibility Criteria: - Organizational and coordination skills - Experience with community work (preferred) - Good interpersonal skills - Access to local resources/networks



Role Description: Design and create Information, Education, and Communication (IEC) materials including posters, infographics, videos, and presentations.
Expectations: - Create 2-3 IEC materials per month - Use design software (Canva, Adobe Suite, etc.) - Ensure materials are culturally appropriate and accessible - Collaborate with content creators and health experts
Benefits: - Graphic design and multimedia skills - Portfolio development - Creative expression opportunities - Certificate of contribution
Eligibility Criteria: - Basic design skills or willingness to learn - Access to design software - Understanding of health communication principles - Attention to detail



Role Description: Provide online consultations for patients through our telehealth platform. Guide patients on public health issues and basic medical advice.
Expectations: - Conduct 4-8 consultation sessions per week - Maintain detailed consultation logs - Follow ethical guidelines and privacy standards - Refer complex cases to appropriate specialists
Benefits: - Clinical experience and skill enhancement - Professional development in telehealth - Networking with healthcare professionals - Certificate of contribution and CME credits (if applicable)
Eligibility Criteria: - Qualified healthcare professional (MBBS, Nursing, etc.) - Valid medical license - Experience in patient consultation - Comfortable with digital tools and online platforms



Role Description: Manage online OPD appointments, coordinate consultation schedules, and maintain patient records.
Expectations: - Schedule and manage appointments - Coordinate with consultants and patients - Maintain accurate logs and records - Handle technical issues with the platform
Benefits: - Administrative and coordination skills - Experience in healthcare management - Understanding of telehealth operations - Certificate of contribution
Eligibility Criteria: - Strong organizational skills - Experience with scheduling software - Good communication skills - Basic understanding of healthcare processes




We’re launching our online OPD service using Google Meet for video consultations. Here’s how it works:



Book Appointment: Use our online booking system to schedule a consultation
Receive Link: Get a personalized Google Meet link via email/SMS
Join Consultation: Connect at the scheduled time for your virtual appointment
Get Prescription: Receive digital prescriptions and follow-up instructions





Check Schedule: View your assigned consultation slots
Join Meeting: Use the provided Google Meet link
Consult: Provide professional medical advice
Document: Maintain detailed consultation logs





We’re using a dedicated Google account for NGO activities
Google Meet links are embedded in our website for seamless access
Appointments are managed through an integrated calendar system





For digital prescriptions, we recommend the following approach:



Use standardized templates in Google Docs or Microsoft Word
Include patient details, diagnosis, medications, and instructions
Email prescriptions to patients with digital signatures





Consider free/open-source prescription software
Integrate with our website for direct prescription generation
Ensure compliance with local medical regulations





Explore partnerships with established telemedicine services
Use their prescription management systems
Maintain data privacy and security

We’re currently evaluating the best option that complies with Indian medical regulations and our NGO’s capabilities.




Interested in volunteering? Here’s how to get started:

Review Opportunities: Read the role descriptions above
Prepare Application: Send us your resume/CV and a brief cover letter
Contact Us: Email volunteer@epipulse.org with your application
Interview: We’ll schedule a quick chat to discuss your interests
Orientation: Join our volunteer orientation session
Get Started: Begin contributing to our mission!





Commitment: We expect consistent participation based on your agreed schedule
Confidentiality: All patient and organizational information must be kept confidential
Ethics: Follow ethical guidelines in all interactions
Communication: Regular updates and feedback are essential
Professionalism: Maintain professional conduct in all volunteer activities




For volunteer inquiries: - Email: volunteer@epipulse.org - Phone: [Contact number] - Website: [Contact form link]
Join us in making a difference in public health! Your skills and passion can help transform communities and save lives.

title: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis (SRMA) Tutorial
text:
This tutorial provides a comprehensive, step-by-step guide to conducting systematic reviews and meta-analyses (SRMA) in public health research. It follows the methodology outlined in the Cochrane Handbook for Systematic Reviews of Interventions.
Systematic reviews involve a rigorous, transparent process to identify, evaluate, and synthesize all relevant studies on a particular topic. Meta-analysis is the statistical combination of results from multiple studies to provide a more precise estimate of effect.


Before starting, ensure you have R and RStudio installed. You’ll need the following packages:
install.packages(c("revtools", "dplyr", "stringr", "metagear", "meta", "metafor", "PRISMAstatement"))

Notes
This tutorial provides a simplified overview. In practice, each step requires careful consideration and expertise. Always consult with methodologists and follow the latest guidelines. The R code examples are illustrative and may need adaptation for your specific research question and data.

text:
Academic Objective
EpiPulse resources are developed to:

Strengthen protocol-to-publication research workflows
Improve evidence quality in public health decision-making
Build practical skills in epidemiology, analytics, and implementation
Standardize documentation, data governance, and reproducibility
Support collaborative work across research, program, and systems teams

title: Public Health Calendar
text:
Stay informed about important health observances, awareness campaigns, and public health events throughout the year. This calendar highlights key dates when communities focus on specific health issues and prevention strategies.




January 12 - National Youth Day - Focus on youth health and wellness - Mental health awareness for young people - Substance abuse prevention programs
January 24 - National Girl Child Day - Focus: Girl child education and health - Activities: - Awareness about gender equality - Nutrition programs for girls - Health screening camps for girls
January 30 - World Leprosy Eradication Day - Objectives: - Raise awareness about leprosy elimination - Reduce stigma and discrimination - Promote early diagnosis and treatment - Activities: Community screening camps, public education, healthcare provider training




February 4 - World Cancer Day - Theme: Focus on cancer prevention, early detection, and treatment - Objectives: - Reduce preventable deaths - Increase health awareness - Encourage healthy lifestyle choices - Activities: - Health camps and screening - Public awareness seminars - Educational material distribution
February 6 - Female Genital Mutilation (FGM) Zero Tolerance Day - Focus on eliminating harmful practices - Women’s health advocacy
February 9 - International Epilepsy Day - Focus: Epilepsy awareness and management - Activities: Awareness campaigns, counseling services
February 10 - National Deworming Day (India) - Focus: Parasitic disease prevention - Target: School children and vulnerable populations - Activities: Mass deworming campaigns, health education
February 11 - Women in Science Day - Recognize female scientists and healthcare professionals - Encourage women in STEM and health fields
February 20 - Social Justice Day (India) - Focus on health equity and inclusive healthcare - Community health initiatives
February 28 - National Science Day (India) - Promote scientific awareness in health - Science-based health communication




March 3 - World Hearing Day - Focus: Ear and hearing health - Activities: Hearing screening camps, awareness about noise pollution
March 8 - International Women’s Day - Focus on women’s health and empowerment - Maternal health, reproductive health, workplace safety - Activities: - Health camps for women - Awareness about cervical cancer, breast cancer screening - Women’s nutrition and wellness programs
March 12 - World Kidney Day - Focus: Kidney disease prevention and management - Activities: Screening for chronic kidney disease, health education
March 16 - National Vaccination Day (India) - Focus: Immunization awareness and coverage - Activities: Vaccination camps, parent education
March 20 - World Oral Health Day - Focus: Dental health and hygiene - Activities: Oral health screening, dental care education
March 21 - Down Syndrome Day - Focus: Awareness about Down Syndrome and inclusivity - Activities: Community awareness, support for families
March 22 - World Water Day - Focus: Water safety and sanitation for health - Clean water access, disease prevention
March 24 - World Tuberculosis Day - Objectives: Eliminate TB through prevention and treatment - Focus Areas: - Increase diagnosis rates - Promote adherence to treatment - Combat drug-resistant TB - Activities: - Community TB screening - Patient education - Healthcare provider training - Public awareness campaigns
March 25 - International Day for Unborn Child - Focus on prenatal health and fetal development - Maternal nutrition and care
March 26 - Epilepsy Awareness Day - Reduce stigma associated with epilepsy - Treatment and management awareness




April 1 - Prevention of Blindness Week - Focus: Eyesight protection and prevention of blindness - Activities: Eye screening camps, awareness about eye health
April 2 - World Autism Awareness Day - Focus: Improve quality of life for people with autism - Activities: - School and community programs - Parent support groups - Healthcare provider education
April 7 - World Health Day - Annual Theme: Varies yearly (check WHO website) - Objectives: Promote global health priorities - Activities: - Major health campaigns - Ministry of Health initiatives - Public health programs - Community engagement
April 11 - Safe Motherhood Day - Focus: Maternal health and safe delivery - Activities: Antenatal care camps, skilled birth attendance programs
April 17 - Haemophilia Day - Focus: Awareness of bleeding disorders - Activities: Support groups, treatment access advocacy
April 22 - Earth Day - Environmental health and sustainability - Impact on public health
April 25 - World Malaria Day - Focus: Malaria prevention and control - Activities: - Mass distribution of bed nets - Community education - Testing and treatment camps
April 28 - Occupational Safety Day - Focus: Workplace health and safety - Activities: Occupational health programs, safety awareness




May 5 - World Asthma Day - Focus: Asthma management and prevention - Activities: Screening camps, breathing exercises, inhaler technique training
May 8 - Thalassemia Day - Focus: Awareness about thalassemia and blood disorders - Activities: Support groups, transfusion services awareness
May 10 - Lupus Day - Focus: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) awareness - Activities: Patient education and support
May 12 - International Nurses Day - Recognize nursing profession - Focus on role in public health
May 15 - Family Day - Focus: Family health and wellness - Activities: Family health camps and counseling
May 17 - Hypertension Day - Focus: Blood pressure management and control - Activities: BP screening, dietary counseling
May 28 - Menstrual Hygiene Day - Focus: Period health and menstrual hygiene - Activities: Awareness about hygiene products, addressing stigma
May 31 - World No Tobacco Day - Theme: Emphasis on tobacco control - Objectives: - Reduce tobacco use - Address secondhand smoke - Combat tobacco industry marketing - Activities: - Community awareness programs - Smoking cessation workshops - School-based programs - Public pledges against tobacco




June 1 - CPR Awareness Week - Focus: Cardiopulmonary resuscitation training - Activities: CPR training camps, first aid workshops
June 4 - Day of Innocent Children Victims of Aggression - Focus: Child protection and safety - Activities: Child abuse prevention programs
June 5 - World Environment Day - Environmental health and sustainability - Impact of pollution on health
June 7 - World Food Safety Day - Focus: Safe food handling and foodborne illness prevention - Activities: Food safety awareness, hygiene programs
June 8 - Brain Tumor Day - Focus: Brain tumor awareness and support - Activities: Awareness campaigns, support groups
June 14 - World Blood Donor Day - Focus: Blood safety and availability - Activities: - Blood donation camps - Blood storage and safety education - Donor recruitment drives
June 15 - World Elder Abuse Awareness Day - Focus: Protection of elderly persons - Activities: Elderly care programs, awareness campaigns
June 18 - Autistic Pride Day - Focus: Celebration of autism and neurodiversity - Activities: Community awareness and inclusion programs
June 19 - Sickle Cell Day - Focus: Sickle cell disease awareness - Activities: Screening for hemoglobinopathies, patient support
June 21 - International Yoga Day - Focus: Physical fitness and mental wellness - Activities: - Mass yoga sessions - Community wellness programs - School-based programs
June 26 - International Day Against Drug Abuse - Focus: Substance abuse prevention - Activities: Awareness campaigns, rehabilitation services
June 27 - HIV Testing Day - Focus: HIV awareness and testing - Activities: Confidential testing camps, counseling services

July 11 - World Population Day - Family planning and reproductive health - Population dynamics and sustainability
July 28 - World Hepatitis Day - Focus: Viral hepatitis prevention - Objectives: - Eliminate hepatitis through vaccination - Improve diagnosis and treatment - Activities: - Screening camps - Vaccination drives - Health education

title: Online Consultations - Google Meet
text:
Welcome to EpiPulse’s online consultation platform. We provide secure, accessible healthcare consultations through Google Meet, allowing you to connect with healthcare professionals from the comfort of your home.







Visit our Book Appointment page
Fill out your details and health concern
Select your preferred date, time, and service type
Submit the form





You’ll receive a confirmation email/SMS within 24 hours
Confirmation includes:

Appointment date and time
Doctor/consultant name
Google Meet link for the consultation
Meeting code and instructions






Check your internet connection (minimum 2 Mbps recommended)
Find a quiet, private space
Ensure adequate lighting for video
Have any medical documents/reports ready
Join 5-10 minutes before the scheduled time




Option A: Click the Link - Click the Google Meet link in your confirmation email - Browser will open Google Meet - Grant camera and microphone permissions - Click “Join Now”
Option B: Use Meeting Code - Go to meet.google.com - Click “Join or start a meeting” - Enter the meeting code provided - Click “Join”




Greet your doctor when they join
Discuss your health concern
Follow medical advice provided
Ask clarifying questions
Take notes on recommendations





Your doctor will provide:

Digital prescription via email
Consultation notes
Follow-up instructions
Any referrals if needed










Use Dedicated Account

Use provided Google account credentials
Check calendar for scheduled appointments
Prepare meeting links in advance

Generate/Verify Meet Link

For each appointment, have the Meet code ready
Test audio/video 5 minutes before
Ensure good lighting and background

Join Meeting

Join using the meeting code
Greet patient professionally
Conduct consultation following medical protocols

Document Consultation

Take detailed notes in patient record
Record vital information
Document diagnosis and treatment plan

Issue Prescription

Create digital prescription
Email to patient with follow-up instructions
Include referrals if needed
Update appointment status as “Completed”










Minimum Specifications: - Internet: Broadband connection (2 Mbps minimum, 4 Mbps recommended) - Device: Computer, tablet, or smartphone - Browser: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge (latest versions) - Camera: Built-in or external webcam - Microphone: Built-in or external microphone - Audio: Speakers or headphones



✅ Desktop/Laptop - Windows, Mac, Linux - Any modern browser
✅ Tablets - iPad, Android tablets - Google Meet app recommended
✅ Smartphones - iPhone/iOS - Android phones - Google Meet app recommended






Activity
Required Speed




Video call
2.5 - 4 Mbps


Screen sharing
3 - 4 Mbps


Multiple participants
5+ Mbps











Video & Audio

Crystal clear HD video
Noise cancellation
Multiple microphone options

Screen Sharing

Doctor can share medical documents
Patient can share reports
Easy reference during consultation

Recording (Optional)

Patient can request recording for reference
Consent required from both parties
Stored securely for future reference

Chat

Backup communication if audio fails
Share follow-up information
Exchange contact details if needed

Hand Raise

Patient can signal questions
Use for getting doctor’s attention










Confirm appointment date and time in your email/SMS
Test internet connection on the device you’ll use
Install Google Meet app (recommended for mobile)
Find a quiet, private space
Gather any medical documents or reports
Charge your device or keep it plugged in
Plan 5 minutes buffer before appointment time
Review your health symptoms/concerns
Have pen and paper ready for notes
Prepare list of medications you’re currently taking




Before your appointment, test your connection: 1. Visit speedtest.net 2. Run the test 3. Check if download speed is at least 2.5 Mbps 4. If slower, try: - Moving closer to WiFi router - Closing other apps using internet - Using wired connection if possible - Restarting your router







I Can’t Hear the Doctor - Check microphone is not muted (unmute button at bottom) - Check speaker volume - Try wearing headphones - Restart the meeting
Doctor Can’t Hear Me - Check microphone is working in Meet settings - Test microphone before appointment - Ensure no apps are blocking microphone - Try different microphone if available
Video Not Working - Allow camera permissions in browser - Check if webcam is blocked by other app - Try different browser - Restart computer
Poor Video Quality - Move closer to WiFi router - Close other apps using internet - Reduce video quality in Meet settings - Check for WiFi interference (move away from microwave, etc.)
Can’t Find Meeting Link - Check email spam/junk folder - Request link to be resent - Call us: [Phone number] - Check your booking confirmation page
Wrong Device Name Showing - Disconnect other devices - Update browser/app - Restart Google Meet - Log out and log back in
Lag/Delay in Video - Check internet speed - Close unnecessary browser tabs - Restart WiFi router - Move to stronger WiFi signal area



If issues persist: - Email: support@epipulse.org - Phone: [Your phone number] - WhatsApp: [Your WhatsApp number] - Hours: 9 AM - 6 PM, Monday to Saturday








Encryption: Google Meet uses end-to-end encryption
Privacy: Only you and the doctor can see/hear
Recording: Only with explicit consent
Data Storage: Secure servers with backup
HIPAA Compliant: Follows healthcare privacy standards
No Third-Party Access: Your data is not shared





✅ Join from a private location
✅ Use a strong password (if set)
✅ Report any security concerns immediately
❌ Don’t share your meeting link with others
❌ Don’t record without permission
❌ Don’t join from shared/public devices without caution








Please Do: - ✅ Arrive 5-10 minutes early - ✅ Dress appropriately - ✅ Maintain eye contact (look at camera) - ✅ Speak clearly and directly - ✅ Be respectful and honest about symptoms - ✅ Ask for clarifications - ✅ Take notes on advice
Please Don’t: - ❌ Multitask during consultation - ❌ Have distracting backgrounds - ❌ Use offensive language - ❌ Interrupt the doctor - ❌ Share personal information unnecessarily - ❌ Involve too many people in the consultation - ❌ Record without consent








Days: Monday to Saturday
Hours: 9 AM - 6 PM
Duration: 30 minutes
Fee: ₹200





Days: Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday
Hours: 10 AM - 4 PM
Duration: 45 minutes
Fee: ₹300





Days: Monday to Friday
Hours: 9 AM - 5 PM
Duration: 30 minutes
Fee: ₹250





Days: Monday to Saturday
Hours: 9 AM - 2 PM
Duration: 30 minutes
Fee: ₹250









Cancel/Reschedule: Up to 24 hours before appointment
No Show: 2 consecutive no-shows may result in booking suspension
Emergency Cancellation: Call us immediately





Email: appointments@epipulse.org
Phone: [Your phone number]
WhatsApp: [Your WhatsApp number]
Mention your appointment ID









Review Prescription

Read digital prescription carefully
Follow medication instructions
Note any precautions

Follow Medical Advice

Implement lifestyle changes suggested
Take medications as prescribed
Complete any recommended tests

Book Follow-up

If doctor recommended follow-up, book next appointment
Usually within 2-4 weeks

Seek Emergency Help If:

Symptoms worsen significantly
New severe symptoms develop
Allergic reaction to medication
Call 108 or go to nearest hospital

Share Feedback

Help us improve the service
Email feedback to: feedback@epipulse.org
Your input is valuable!







Q: Is Google Meet secure for medical consultations? A: Yes. Google Meet uses industry-standard encryption and is HIPAA-compliant.
Q: Can someone else be present during my consultation? A: Family members can be present with your knowledge, but the consultation is confidential.
Q: What if I miss my appointment? A: Notify us within 24 hours to reschedule. Repeated no-shows may result in restrictions.
Q: Can I get a recorded copy of the consultation? A: Only if you request recording before the appointment. Both parties must consent.
Q: What payment methods do you accept? A: Currently cash/bank transfer. Contact us for payment options.
Q: Is the prescription valid legally? A: Yes, digital prescriptions from our licensed doctors are legally valid in India.
Q: Can I consult with a specific doctor? A: Request during booking. Subject to availability.
Q: What happens during technical failure? A: Doctor will attempt to reconnect. If unable, a callback will be arranged.




Book Your Appointment Now





Email: support@epipulse.org
Phone: [Your phone number]
WhatsApp: [Your WhatsApp number]
Hours: 9 AM - 6 PM, Monday to Saturday

Your health, our priority. Consult with confidence!

title: EpiPulse
text:
Section 8 Public Health Organization



EpiPulse is building a mission-driven public health platform that links epidemiology, implementation science, and community partnerships to create measurable social benefit.
Mission and Vision Volunteer With Us

title: EpiPulse
text:
Mission, Vision, and Objectives


Mission
Generate and translate high-quality public health evidence into practical decisions, responsible implementation, and community benefit.


Vision
Build healthier, resilient, and equitable communities by connecting science, systems, and people-centered action.


Strategic Objective
Create an institution that is scientifically rigorous, ethically grounded, operationally transparent, and collaborative by design.

title: EpiPulse
text:
Core Objectives


Objective 01
Advance epidemiology and implementation research focused on real public health priorities.


Objective 02
Strengthen prevention and health-promotion programs in community settings.


Objective 03
Support institutions with training, methods, and technical public health systems inputs.


Objective 04
Embed ethics, equity, One Health thinking, and data responsibility across all workflows.

title: EpiPulse
text:
How the Mission Gets Delivered
Our mission is executed through eight internal divisions:

Research and Evidence
Public Health Implementation
Collaboration and Partnerships
Accounts and Legal Compliance
Software and Systems
Education and Fellowship
Monitoring, Evaluation, Impact, and Ethics
Grants, Funding, and Resource Mobilization

Explore division-aligned materials in the Resource Hub.

title: AI-assisted Data Cleaning Workflow
text:
This site documents a reproducible, AI-assisted workflow for public health data analysis with R.



AI-assisted code generation
Data cleaning and validation, variable labeling and reference categories
Exporting data for R, SPSS, and Stata


⚠️ This is a template document.







R is the core statistical computing environment used for data cleaning and analysis.
Visit the Comprehensive R Archive Network (CRAN): https://cran.r-project.org
Download and install R for your operating system:

Windows
macOS
Linux

Accept default installation settings.
After installation, verify R by opening it and running:

version




RStudio is an integrated development environment (IDE) that makes working with R scripts, projects, and Quarto documents easier.
Download RStudio Desktop (Free version): https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/
Install RStudio after R is installed.
Open RStudio and confirm it detects your R installation.



Run the following once inside RStudio:

install.packages(c(
  "dplyr",
  "janitor",
  "skimr",
  "jsonlite",
  "ellmer",
  "haven",
  "labelled",
  "rio",
  "ollamar"
))




For environments where cloud-based AI tools are restricted (for example hospitals and government institutions), a local large language model (LLM) can be used. This workflow supports Ollama, which runs entirely offline.



Download Ollama from: https://ollama.com
Install Ollama for your operating system (Windows/macOS/Linux).
After installation, verify Ollama is running by opening a terminal and typing:
ollama --version



Gemma is a lightweight, open-source model suitable for structured code generation tasks.
In your system terminal, run:

ollama run codegemma

(You may also use larger variants if system resources permit.)



Once Ollama is running in the background, test the connection from R:

library(ellmer)

model <- chat_ollama(
  model = "codegemma"
)

chat(model, "Respond with: Ollama is working")

If successful, the model will return a short confirmation message.

title: AI-assisted Data Cleaning Workflow
text:
2.1 AI Data Cleaning

2.1.1 Statement to add in methodology
“AI-assisted code generation was used for data cleaning, with all scripts reviewed and executed by the investigators.”
Always de-identify data before sending anything to AI.


2.1.2 Load and summary for AI

library(dplyr)
library(janitor)
library(skimr)
library(jsonlite)

df <- read.csv("data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
df <- clean_names(df)

data_summary <- list(
  structure = str(df),
  missing_pct = round(colMeans(is.na(df)) * 100, 2),
  unique_character_values = df |>
    select(where(is.character)) |>
    summarise(across(everything(), ~ paste(unique(.), collapse = ", "))),
  numeric_ranges = df |>
    select(where(is.numeric)) |>
    summarise(across(everything(), ~ paste(range(., na.rm = TRUE), collapse = " - ")))
)

summary_text <- toJSON(data_summary, pretty = TRUE)

title: AI-assisted Data Cleaning Workflow
text:
2.2 Setup model and API key

2.2.1 OpenAI

Sys.setenv(OPENAI_API_KEY = "paste_api_key")

library(ellmer)

ai_generate_cleaning_code <- function(summary_text, model) {
  prompt <- paste0(
    "You are a public health data analyst.\n\n",
    "INSTRUCTIONS:\n",
    "- Generate ONLY executable R code\n",
    "- No explanations outside code\n",
    "- Do NOT load libraries\n",
    "- Do NOT delete rows\n",
    "- Do NOT impute values\n",
    "- Do NOT change outcome definitions\n",
    "- Include comments in the R code\n\n",
    "TASKS:\n",
    "- Trim whitespace in character variables\n",
    "- Standardize categorical labels\n",
    "- Convert variables to appropriate types\n",
    "- Flag impossible numeric values\n",
    "- Create missingness flags (not imputation)\n\n",
    "DATA SUMMARY:\n",
    summary_text
  )

  response <- model$chat(prompt)
  response$content
}

prompt <- paste("Use the specified cleaning instructions and return R code only.")

response <- chat_openai(
  model = "gpt-4.1-mini",
  params = list(temperature = 0.1)
)$chat(prompt)

cleaning_code <- response$content
cat(cleaning_code)



2.2.2 Local LLM (offline, hospital-safe)
No API key required for local LLM.

library(ollamar)
library(jsonlite)

ai_generate_cleaning_code <- function(summary_text,
                                      model_name = "codegemma") {

  messages <- list(
    list(
      role = "user",
      content = paste0(
        "INSTRUCTIONS:\n",
        "- Generate ONLY executable R code\n",
        "- No explanations outside code\n",
        "- Do NOT load libraries\n",
        "- Do NOT delete rows\n",
        "- Do NOT impute values\n",
        "- Do NOT change outcome definitions\n",
        "- Include comments in the R code\n",
        "TASKS:\n",
        "- Trim whitespace in character variables\n",
        "- Standardize categorical labels\n",
        "- Convert variables to appropriate types\n",
        "- Flag impossible numeric values\n",
        "- Create missingness flags (not imputation)\n",
        "DATA SUMMARY:\n",
        summary_text
      )
    )
  )

  resp <- ollamar::chat(
    model = model_name,
    messages = messages
  )

  json <- jsonlite::fromJSON(rawToChar(resp$body))
  code <- json$message$content

  if (is.null(code) || code == "") {
    stop("Model returned empty content")
  }

  code <- gsub("^```[rR]?\\s*|```$", "", code)
  code
}

cleaning_code <- ai_generate_cleaning_code(summary_text)
cat(cleaning_code)



2.2.3 Execute after review

eval(parse(text = cleaning_code))

title: AI-assisted Data Cleaning Workflow
section: 2.3 AI Data Typing + Labels + Reference Categories
text:
2.3 AI Data Typing + Labels + Reference Categories
Mention this in methods (recommended):
“AI-assisted code generation was used to standardize variable types, labels, and reference categories. All code was reviewed and executed by the investigators.”

2.3.1 Load function

library(ollamar)
library(jsonlite)

ai_generate_typing_and_labels_code <- function(summary_text,
                                               model_name = "codegemma") {

  prompt <- paste0(
"You are a senior public health biostatistician and R instructor.

TASK:
Generate ONLY executable R code (no explanations outside code).

AUDIENCE:
Public health beginners (MBBS / MPH / MD Community Medicine).

REQUIREMENTS:
1. Ensure correct data types:
   - numeric -> as.numeric
   - categorical -> factor
2. Add VARIABLE LABELS (column descriptions)
3. Add VALUE LABELS for categorical variables
4. Explicitly set REFERENCE CATEGORIES using relevel()
5. Comment clearly why each reference category is chosen
6. Do NOT delete rows
7. Do NOT impute missing values
8. Do NOT load libraries
9. Assume dataframe name is `df`

REFERENCE CATEGORY RULES:
- Sex -> reference = \"Male\"
- Binary exposure -> reference = \"No\" / \"Unexposed\"
- Education -> lowest level
- Socioeconomic status -> lowest class
- Outcome variables -> DO NOT relevel

OUTPUT:
Return ONLY R code with clear comments.

DATA SUMMARY:
", summary_text
  )

  messages <- list(
    list(role = "user", content = prompt)
  )

  resp <- ollamar::chat(
    model = model_name,
    messages = messages
  )

  json <- jsonlite::fromJSON(rawToChar(resp$body))
  code <- json$message$content

  if (is.null(code) || code == "") {
    stop("Model returned empty content")
  }

  code <- gsub("^```[rR]?\\s*|```$", "", code)
  code
}



2.3.2 Generate the code

typing_label_code <- ai_generate_typing_and_labels_code(summary_text)
cat(typing_label_code)



2.3.3 Execute code after review

eval(parse(text = typing_label_code))

title: AI-assisted Data Cleaning Workflow
text:
3.1 Data inspection

3.1.1 Load libraries

library(dplyr)
library(janitor)
library(skimr)



3.1.2 Load data

df <- read.csv("data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)



3.1.3 Inspect

dim(df); names(df); str(df); summary(df)



3.1.4 Clean names

df <- clean_names(df)



3.1.5 Explore

skim(df)



3.1.6 Unique values

df |> select(where(is.character)) |> lapply(unique)



3.1.7 Missingness

colMeans(is.na(df)) * 100



3.1.8 Validate

table(df$sex)
range(df$age, na.rm = TRUE)

title: AI-assisted Data Cleaning Workflow
text:
3.2 Data typing + labels + reference categories (sample)

# Ensure age is numeric
df <- df |>
  mutate(age = as.numeric(age))

# Convert sex to factor and set reference category
# Reference = Male (standard epidemiologic practice)
df <- df |>
  mutate(
    sex = factor(sex, levels = c("Male", "Female")),
    sex = relevel(sex, ref = "Male")
  )

# Convert smoking status to factor
# Reference = No (unexposed group)
df <- df |>
  mutate(
    smoking = factor(smoking, levels = c("No", "Yes")),
    smoking = relevel(smoking, ref = "No")
  )

# Education level (reference = lowest education)
df <- df |>
  mutate(
    education = factor(
      education,
      levels = c("No formal education", "Primary", "Secondary", "Higher")
    ),
    education = relevel(education, ref = "No formal education")
  )

# Add variable labels (for tables and reports)
attr(df$age, "label") <- "Age in completed years"
attr(df$sex, "label") <- "Sex of the participant"
attr(df$smoking, "label") <- "Current smoking status"
attr(df$education, "label") <- "Highest educational attainment"

title: Manage Your Appointments
text:
PENDING 🟡 - Your appointment request received - Awaiting final confirmation - We’ll contact you within 24 hours - Action: Confirm you can attend
CONFIRMED 🟢 - Appointment is confirmed - You have the Google Meet link - Doctor is assigned - Action: Prepare and join on time
IN PROGRESS 🔵 - Consultation is happening now - Doctor is online and waiting - Join the Google Meet meeting - Action: Click the Meet link
COMPLETED ✅ - Consultation finished - Prescription sent via email - Consultation notes forwarded - Action: Follow medical advice
CANCELLED ❌ - Appointment was cancelled - No charge if cancelled 24hrs before - You can rebook anytime - Action: Book new appointment
NO-SHOW 🚫 - You didn’t join the meeting - Doctor waited but consultation didn’t happen - May affect future bookings - Action: Contact us to explain

title: Manage Your Appointments
text:
Q: Can I book multiple appointments at once? A: Yes, use the booking form multiple times or contact us for bulk booking.
Q: Can someone else attend my appointment? A: Family can be present, but consultation code is confidential to you.
Q: What if I’m late to join? A: Join within 15 minutes. After that, slot considered no-show.
Q: Can I reschedule multiple times? A: Yes, if done within 24 hours of each appointment.
Q: What if the doctor doesn’t show up? A: We’ll contact you within 15 minutes to reschedule. No charge.
Q: Can I get consultation in my language? A: Request during booking. Availability depends on doctor.
Q: Is my health information confidential? A: Yes, 100% confidential. Follows medical privacy laws.
Q: Can I share my appointment link with family? A: Not recommended. Your appointment is personal and confidential.

title: Railway Deployment Guide
text:
This guide walks you through deploying EpiPulse’s custom appointment booking system on Railway - a free, easy-to-use cloud hosting platform perfect for NGOs.


Railway is a free hosting platform that: - ✅ Hosts your Node.js application - ✅ Provides free PostgreSQL database - ✅ Offers automatic deployments from GitHub - ✅ Includes free SSL/HTTPS certificate - ✅ Gives you $5 credit monthly - ✅ No credit card required initially
Alternative: You can also use the simpler Formspree solution for basic form submissions.







Complete Control: Your own server, your own data
Free: $5/month credit covers small NGO operations
Scalable: Grows with your organization
Easy to Update: Deploy changes in seconds
Database Included: PostgreSQL for patient records
Professional: Custom domain support





Requires GitHub account and basic Git knowledge
Setup takes 30-45 minutes
Needs some technical understanding









GitHub Account (free)

Sign up at github.com
Upload our appointment code

Railway Account (free)

Sign up at railway.app
Link with GitHub

Text Editor

VS Code (recommended, free)
Notepad++, Sublime Text, or any editor

Git (optional but recommended)

Download from git-scm.com
Allows easy code synchronization










Create GitHub Account

Go to github.com
Sign up with email
Verify email

Create New Repository

Click “+” in top right
Select “New repository”
Name: epipulse-appointments
Description: “EpiPulse Online Appointment Booking System”
Select “Public”
Check “Add README”
Click “Create repository”

Clone Repository to Your Computer
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/epipulse-appointments.git
cd epipulse-appointments




Copy these files to your repository folder:
1. package.json
{
  "name": "epipulse-appointments",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "main": "server.js",
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node server.js",
    "dev": "nodemon server.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "express": "^4.18.2",
    "pg": "^8.11.0",
    "ejs": "^3.1.9",
    "body-parser": "^1.20.2",
    "cors": "^2.8.5",
    "dotenv": "^16.3.1",
    "bcryptjs": "^2.4.3",
    "jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.2"
  }
}
2. .env.example
DATABASE_URL=your_database_url_here
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=production
3. server.js (see previous message for full code)
4. public/index.html (see previous message for full code)
5. public/style.css (see previous message for full code)
6. public/script.js (see previous message for full code)
7. views/admin.ejs (see previous message for full code)
8. .gitignore
node_modules/
.env
.DS_Store
*.log



git add .
git commit -m "Initial EpiPulse appointment booking system"
git push origin main








Go to Railway

Visit railway.app
Click “Start a New Project”
Choose “Deploy from GitHub”

Connect GitHub

Click “Connect GitHub”
Authorize Railway to access your GitHub
Select your epipulse-appointments repository

Create Database

Railway will ask to add a service
Select “PostgreSQL”
It creates automatically
Takes 1-2 minutes

Deploy Application

Railway reads package.json
Creates Node.js environment
Automatic deployment begins
Watch the logs





Once deployed:

Go to Project Settings

In Railway dashboard
Click your project
Go to “Variables”

Add Variables (Railway auto-provides DATABASE_URL)
PORT: 3000
NODE_ENV: production
DATABASE_URL: (automatically set)
Save and Deploy

Changes trigger automatic redeployment
Takes 2-3 minutes










Access Railway Database

In Railway, go to PostgreSQL service
Click “Connect”
Note connection details

Run SQL Commands

Use pgAdmin or Railway’s built-in SQL editor
Run the init.sql file content:


-- Create services table
CREATE TABLE services (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  duration_minutes INTEGER DEFAULT 30,
  fee DECIMAL(10,2),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Create appointments table
CREATE TABLE appointments (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  phone VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  email VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  dob DATE,
  gender VARCHAR(20),
  service_id INTEGER REFERENCES services(id),
  preferred_date DATE NOT NULL,
  time_slot VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  health_concern TEXT,
  previous_consultation BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  emergency BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending',
  notes TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Insert services
INSERT INTO services (name, description, duration_minutes, fee) VALUES
('General OPD', 'Primary healthcare consultation', 30, 200.00),
('Deaddiction Consultation', 'Addiction assessment and counseling', 45, 300.00),
('NCD Care', 'Non-communicable disease management', 30, 250.00),
('Pediatric Care', 'Child healthcare services', 30, 250.00);








Edit server.js

Update email settings if needed
Customize responses
Add your organization name

Update public/index.html

Change hospital/NGO name
Update contact information
Customize colors/branding

Update views/admin.ejs

Admin dashboard customization
Add your logo
Customize form fields





git add .
git commit -m "Customize for EpiPulse"
git push origin main
Railway automatically deploys changes!







Add to your services.qmd or dedicated booking page:
<iframe src="https://your-railway-app.railway.app" 
        width="100%" 
        height="800" 
        frameborder="0">
</iframe>
Replace your-railway-app with your actual Railway app URL (found in Railway dashboard).




Add Custom Domain

In Railway dashboard
Go to “Settings”
Add your domain
Follow DNS instructions

Example

Instead of: epipulse-123456.railway.app
Use: appointments.epipulse.org










Book Test Appointment

Fill form with test data
Verify it appears in database
Check admin panel

Check Database

Go to Railway PostgreSQL
Query: SELECT * FROM appointments;
Verify data saved correctly

Test Notifications

Set up email notifications (optional)
Deploy email plugin
Test with real data






Railway dashboard shows:

Application logs
Database usage
Deployment history
Performance metrics









View Appointments
Admin Panel: /admin
Lists all bookings
Status: Pending, Confirmed, Completed
Update Status - Pending → Confirmed (when scheduling doctor) - Confirmed → Completed (after consultation) - Any → Cancelled (if patient cancels)



To add admin authentication:

Update server.js

// Add login route
app.post('/admin/login', (req, res) => {
  // Verify credentials
  // Start session
  // Redirect to admin panel
});

Update views/admin.ejs

<!-- Add login form -->
<form method="POST" action="/admin/login">
  <input type="email" name="email" required>
  <input type="password" name="password" required>
  <button type="submit">Login</button>
</form>







Problem: Railway shows deployment errors
Solution: - Check logs in Railway dashboard - Ensure package.json is correct - Verify GitHub repo is public - Check Node.js version compatibility



Problem: “Cannot connect to database”
Solution: - Verify DATABASE_URL in variables - Check PostgreSQL service is running - Ensure tables are created - Restart application from Railway



Problem: Booking form not working
Solution: - Check browser console for errors - Verify server.js is running - Test database connection - Check CORS settings



Problem: App keeps restarting
Solution: - Check error logs - Verify environment variables set - Ensure port 3000 is available - Review server.js for syntax errors







Monthly: - Check Railway logs for errors - Backup database - Review bookings
Quarterly: - Update dependencies (npm update) - Review and optimize code - Check application performance



# Export data
pg_dump -U username dbname > backup.sql

# Import data
psql -U username dbname < backup.sql







Monthly credits: $5
Daily spending: ~$0.15-0.30

Includes:
- Node.js application hosting
- PostgreSQL database (5GB)
- 100GB bandwidth (per month)
- SSL certificate
- Automatic backups

For EpiPulse:
- ~50-100 appointments/month
- 1GB database space used
- Cost: **COMPLETELY FREE**



If you exceed free tier: - Pay-as-you-go pricing - Transparent billing - No hidden charges - Easy to scale up/down





If Railway seems too complex, use Formspree:
Advantages: - ✅ No server setup required - ✅ No coding knowledge needed - ✅ 50 submissions/month free - ✅ Email notifications automatic - ✅ Quick 5-minute setup
Disadvantages: - Only basic form submissions - No patient database - Limited to 50 submissions/month - No advanced features
Setup Time: 5 minutes Vs Railway Setup Time: 45 minutes







You want your own appointment database
You need advanced features (patient history, automated confirmations)
You want complete control and independence
You plan to scale significantly
You want professional presentation





You want quick setup (5 minutes vs 45 minutes)
You don’t need patient database
You’re comfortable managing data in spreadsheets
You want zero technical complexity
You just starting out







Create GitHub Account → github.com
Create Railway Account → railway.app
Follow Step 1-3 Above → Complete in 30 minutes
Test System → Book test appointment
Go Live → Add to your website

Need Help? - Email: tech-support@epipulse.org - Consult: Railway Documentation - GitHub: Railway GitHub

Your appointment system, your way, for free!

title: Our Services
text:
EpiPulse provides accessible healthcare services through our online OPD platform. We offer general consultations and specialized care in key areas of public health concern. All consultations are conducted via secure video calls using Google Meet.


Our general OPD provides primary healthcare consultations for common health issues, preventive care, and health education.



Routine health check-ups
Common illness diagnosis and treatment
Health counseling and education
Preventive care advice
Referral to specialists when needed











We offer specialized consultations in areas critical to public health in India.


Comprehensive support for substance use disorders and addiction recovery.



Addiction assessment and counseling
Withdrawal management guidance
Relapse prevention strategies
Family counseling
Referral to rehabilitation centers














Focused care for chronic conditions like diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, and respiratory disorders.



NCD screening and diagnosis
Chronic disease management
Lifestyle modification counseling
Medication adherence support
Complication prevention











Dr. [Name], Diabetologist
Dr. [Name], Cardiologist

title: Transparency
section: Memorandum of Association (MOA): Core Object Areas
text:
Memorandum of Association (MOA): Core Object Areas

1) Research, Evidence, and Community Implementation

Public health and epidemiology research
Implementation and operational research
Evidence synthesis and policy translation
Field-based public health innovation



2) Research Support and Capacity Building

Protocol and study design support
Biostatistics and data services
Manuscript and publication support
Training, workshops, fellowships, internships



3) Public Health Programs and CSR Implementation

Program design and execution
Health promotion and prevention activities
Collaborations with government, academia, NGOs, and private partners



Incidental/Ancillary Objects

National and international collaboration
Receiving lawful grants, donations, and CSR funds
Publishing reports and educational materials
Conducting conferences and technical events

title: Transparency
text:
Articles of Association (AOA): Governance Framework

Membership

Initial members are MOA subscribers
New members require unanimous existing-member approval plus Board approval
No open or automatic membership



Board and Governance

Minimum two directors; up to fifteen as per law
Board manages operations under MOA/AOA
Director removal by special resolution



Founder/President Framework

Founder status is recognized as conceptual origin
President role is governance-advisory, not automatic Board voting authority
No automatic financial privilege from founder/president role



Divisional Structure (Operational)
Planned internal divisions include:

Research and Evidence
Public Health Implementation
Collaboration and Partnerships
Accounts and Legal Compliance
Software and Systems
Education and Fellowship
Monitoring, Evaluation, Impact, and Ethics
Grants, Funding, and Resource Mobilization

title: Transparency
section: Application of Income and Winding Up
text:
Application of Income and Winding Up

Income and property are applied only toward company objects
No dividend/bonus/profit distribution to members
On dissolution, assets transfer to a similar Section 8 entity, not members
